a.other_context: manifest/2026-07-18.md; schemas/synthesis-output.schema.json; schemas/synthesis-item.schema.json; synthesis/README.md; logs/{grok,perplexity,gemini,deepseek,kimi}/prompts.md; tracking/signals.md and tracking/events.jsonl (empty scaffolding, no signals yet); no prior synthesis or readings exist for any cycle

b.other_context: Consulted synthesis/PROMPT.md, schemas/synthesis-output.schema.json, schemas/synthesis-item.schema.json, manifest/2026-07-18.md, the dated capture-file inventory, and git status. Synthesis A existed on GitHub but was not present in the stale local checkout during synthesis and was not read; it was fetched only afterward for publication.

items.rationale: Both syntheses independently identify renewed Gulf conflict as a cross-domain shock transmitted through oil, LNG, ports, freight risk, and inflation. A decomposes the mechanism into four claims; B keeps it integrated. Exact chronology, legal attribution, and extraordinary transit-fee claims remain unresolved and are not treated as convergent facts.

Both independently identify AI data-center load as an explicit driver of transmission planning, grid reliability rules, and advanced-reactor deployment. A includes Eastern corroboration through JERA and tags the claim cross-hemisphere; B limits its core evidence to Western institutions.

Both independently identify Kimi K3's promised open-weight release, coding-benchmark position, and lower-cost framing as pressure on Western frontier-model pricing and margins. A calls the process escalating; B calls it emerging.

Both identify digital money moving toward sovereign and bank-controlled rails. A separates cross-hemisphere state absorption from Eastern non-SWIFT deployment and preserves the U.S.-China interest-policy contrast; B combines the Eastern operational architecture into one claim.

Both identify the Aktau hub and integrated China-Kazakhstan-Caspian network as movement from strategy toward operation. A calls it escalating and emphasizes Western silence; B calls it emerging and emphasizes the need for repeat-throughput proof.

Both identify critical-mineral exposure shifting from ore prices toward licensing, refining control, and sulphuric-acid inputs. A treats licensing and acid disruption as separate verified components; B marks their combined downstream-throughput consequence inferred.

Both independently conclude that the Eastern captures contain irreconcilable China GDP and same-day exchange figures and cannot support a single quantitative conclusion until NBS and exchange records are checked. The conflict itself, not either number, is the convergent finding.

Both distinguish content refusal from unavailable attempts and identify complementary Kimi and DeepSeek failure boundaries. A ledgers each refusal separately; B combines refusals, model changes, and successful retries into a cycle-health claim. These items support collection behavior only, not world claims.
Near-exact independent convergence: both infer that tariff-deadline front-loading is raising freight demand despite softer orders and may produce excess inventory and a late-Q3 freight cliff.

Both infer that persistent migration plus contracting multifamily permits may create localized shelter pressure over 18-24 months. B more explicitly preserves softness in already oversupplied Sun Belt metros.

Both identify China coupling AI capability with international standards-setting. A centers WAICO as an intergovernmental pole; B defines a broader stack spanning domestic compute, regulators, training, and standards programs.

Both identify fusion moving toward commercialization. A centers public-market entry and funding scale and tags the claim Western; B emphasizes drivers, targets, diagnostics, materials computation, and finance and tags the broader stack cross-hemisphere.

Both identify structural Asian consumer divergence from the West. A emphasizes income, geography, services, and demographic bifurcation; B emphasizes social, quick, agent-mediated, and local-brand commerce. The claims overlap but are not interchangeable.

Both find that engagement-ranked social retrieval under-detected logistics stress visible in institutional and operational data. A extends the visibility-gap claim to crypto regulation and market anomalies; B confines its ledger claim to logistics.
Claude alone ledgered Japan's reported crypto-law reclassification, penalties, and future tax treatment. Codex's smaller ledger did not address it; omission is not disagreement.
Claude alone inferred stress in the corporate bitcoin-treasury model. Codex did not ledger an equivalent claim.
Claude alone ledgered a broad global AI and semiconductor equity rotation, especially in Asia. Codex's Kimi K3 item covers one possible margin mechanism but not the broader market claim.
Claude alone ledgered a hawkish communications break under Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. Codex did not ledger an equivalent monetary-policy claim.
Claude alone inferred cross-organization convergence on reasoning efficiency and sparse activation as a competitive axis.
Claude alone ledgered government-negotiated model-release restriction reversals as de facto licensing without a formal regime.
Claude alone ledgered consolidation of Pentagon AI compute among a small provider set and the related congressional disclosure deadline.
Claude alone ledgered Ukraine's reported shift toward economic and logistics targets. Codex did not ledger an equivalent claim.
Claude alone ledgered European defense spending as structural rearmament rather than short-term Ukraine support.
Claude alone ledgered the reported state-level injunction challenge to the Paramount-WBD transaction and its hard ruling date.
Claude alone inferred coordinated Japanese official-capital repositioning through GPIF pressure and JERA's reported U.S. listing study.
Claude alone ledgered Perplexity's domain-06 inability to provide a current official-record read. Codex did not make it a ledger item.
Claude alone ledgered defensive repositioning by Western consumers beneath nominal retail growth.
Claude alone ledgered Dover's reported biometric Entry/Exit System chokepoint ahead of peak summer traffic.

Codex alone ledgered the absence of a substantiated unusual insider-buying signal. Claude's prose notes the same silence but does not provide a ledger item, so the asymmetry is structural rather than substantive.
